title: Kids' Playtable with Wheeled Storage Carts
summary: A low play table with raised side rails to keep toys on it, plus two matching rolling storage boxes that tuck underneath.
description:
This is really two builds sized to work together: a 2x2-framed play table with side rails raised just enough to keep small toys from sliding off the edge, and two open-topped rolling storage boxes on casters that slide in underneath it for cleanup. Building the table's leg-and-apron frame first, exactly as you would any table, then adding the raised rails afterward is what makes the "keep toys on the table" detail simple — it's just a strip of 1x2 held up 1/2" off the tabletop surface, not a routed groove or a raised lip requiring different joinery.

Size the storage carts to actually roll under your table's apron height with clearance for the casters — the plan leaves a 2" gap under the box bottoms specifically for this, so measure your actual casters before finalizing that gap rather than trusting the number blind.
timeLabel: 8–10 hrs for the table and both carts

materials.name: 3/4" hardwood plywood or MDF, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Tabletop and both storage box bottoms.

steps.title: Build the table legs and side aprons
steps.body: Fasten the legs to the side leg support pieces, leaving a 3/4" gap on the ends of each support board. Add the side aprons — if the leg supports are positioned correctly, the aprons will fit against them perfectly. Screw from the leg support and the legs into the side aprons.

steps.title: Add the front and back aprons
steps.body: Add the front and back aprons, screwing into them from the legs and also into the side leg support pieces.

steps.title: Add tabletop supports and the top
steps.body: Evenly space the five tabletop supports across the frame and screw or nail into place. Attach the plywood or MDF tabletop on top.

steps.title: Add the raised side rails
steps.body: Add the side rails to the tabletop, leaving a 1/2" gap above the tabletop surface — this gap is what keeps toys from sliding off. Keep the rails flush at the ends and add a few nails from the rails into the apron below for extra hold. Fasten the front and back rails the same way.

steps.title: Build the storage cart boxes
steps.body: Fasten the front and back of each storage cart to its bottom panel, leaving about a 2" clearance under the bottom for your casters — check your actual caster height and adjust this clearance if needed. Cut the top corners of the fronts and backs if you want a lower profile at the back for reaching in.

steps.title: Finish the storage carts
steps.body: Fasten the sides of each storage cart to the bottom and front/back pieces. Mount the four casters to the underside of each cart, then sand and finish both carts and the table.

images.alt: Kids' play table with raised side rails and two wheeled storage carts underneath
